subject: docs(migrations): record outstanding-migration fix + add filename-uniqueness test
body:
Follow-up to 75b4d67 — documents the drift that was caught (14 of 18
migrations outstanding on remote despite Project Status claiming all
pushed), adds the missing guard rail, and updates the docs that
survive across sessions.

- scripts/__tests__/migration-files.test.ts: Jest test asserting every
  migration filename is a 14-digit timestamp and no two files share a
  version. Would have caught the duplicate 20260311000001 on commit.
  Runs via existing `npm test`.
- CLAUDE.md project-status line: 17/18, with the spatial_ref_sys
  outlier linked to USER_ACTIONS.md.
- CLAUDE.md AI Notes: two new rules — (1) management API and `db push`
  run as `postgres`, not `supabase_admin`, so DDL on PostGIS/auth/
  storage tables must go via Studio SQL Editor; (2) never trust a
  project-status checkbox as evidence of remote state without querying
  the source of truth.
- docs/AI_MISTAKES.md: 2026-04-21 entry — trusted stale checkbox,
  missed duplicate version + return-type change for ~2 months.
- docs/SESSION_NOTES.md: rewritten with this session's actual work.

Skipped deliberately: backfilling `statements` column on the 13
inserted schema_migrations rows (column is nullable, cosmetic only),
and return-type-drift static detection (harder to implement reliably,
lower return than the filename check).

Tags: documentation, migrations, testing
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: db-sync
Semver: patch
